content: I'm setting up a new workstation for my cybersecurity consulting business and YouTube channel. What's the best way to handle dual monitors for both work and video editing?
For dual monitors, you'll want something that can handle both high-resolution displays and intensive video processing. What kind of monitors are you considering?
I was thinking about getting two 27-inch 4K monitors. I just turned 34 and decided it was time to upgrade from my old setup. The YouTube channel is about drone photography tips.
That's a great niche! Two 4K monitors would give you plenty of screen real estate. For the drone footage editing, you'll need a powerful GPU. What graphics card do you have now?
I have an RTX 3060 but I'm thinking of upgrading to an RTX 4080. My current PC is about 3 years old. I also want to start brewing my own beer and documenting the process on the channel.
The 4080 would be excellent for 4K video editing and color grading. Have you considered how you'll manage the audio for your brewing videos? Good sound is crucial for YouTube.
I have a decent USB mic but I might need something better. I'm also getting into speedcubing - trying to get my solve time under 20 seconds. It's a nice break from staring at screens all day.
Do you think I should go with Windows or Linux for my main workstation? I use both for different clients but I'm not sure which should be primary.
For video editing and YouTube production, Windows still has the best software support. You could run Linux in a VM for your cybersecurity work. That way you get the best of both worlds.
That makes sense. I'll probably go with Windows then. I need to order everything this week so I can have it set up before my next consulting project starts.
Sounds like a solid plan. With the right setup, you'll be able to handle both your consulting work and create great content for your channel. Good luck with the speedcubing too!
